value.title: AI companies want you to stop chatting with bots and start managing them
value.content: Claude Opus 4.6 and OpenAI Frontier pitch a future of supervising AI agents.

value.title: OpenAI is hoppin' mad about Anthropic's new Super Bowl TV ads
value.content: Sam Altman calls AI competitor "dishonest" and "authoritarian" in lengthy post on X.

value.title: Increase of AI bots on the Internet sparks arms race
value.content: Publishers are rolling out more aggressive defenses.

value.title: Microsoft releases urgent Office patch. Russian-state hackers pounce.
value.content: The window to patch vulnerabilities is shrinking rapidly.

value.title: Should AI chatbots have ads? Anthropic says no.
value.content: ChatGPT competitor comes out swinging with Super Bowl ad mocking AI product pitches.

value.title: So yeah, I vibe-coded a log colorizer—and I feel good about it
value.content: Some semi-unhinged musings on where LLMs fit into my life—and how I'll keep using them.

value.title: Nvidia's $100 billion OpenAI deal has seemingly vanished
value.content: Two AI giants shake market confidence after investment fails to materialize.

value.title: The rise of Moltbook suggests viral AI prompts may be the next big security threat
value.content: We don't need self-replicating AI models to have problems, just self-replicating prompts.

value.title: Notepad++ users take note: It's time to check if you're hacked
value.content: Suspected China-state hackers used update infrastructure to deliver backdoored version.

value.title: AI agents now have their own Reddit-style social network, and it's getting weird fast
value.content: Moltbook lets 32,000 AI bots trade jokes, tips, and complaints about humans.

value.title: Developers say AI coding tools work—and that's precisely what worries them
value.content: Ars spoke to several software devs about AI and found enthusiasm tempered by unease.
